title: Tracking LLMs in the Wild
concept: Monitoring LLMs in production requires tracking both standard system health (latency, cost) and semantic health (hallucinations, bias).

exercises.prompt: When an AI system is running, do we need to monitor the actual words it outputs?

options.text: No, computers never make language mistakes.
Yes, because AI can generate fake facts or offensive text while running perfectly fast.
Yes, but only to check if the font size is correct.

exercises.explain: Just because an AI server is online doesn't mean the AI is speaking correctly. We must monitor the actual text it writes.

cards.title: AI in the Wild
cards.body: Once you launch an AI chatbot into the real world, how do you know if it's behaving? Unlike normal software, AI can start confidently making up fake facts (hallucinating) or getting slower and costing you thousands of dollars!

cards.title: What is LLM Monitoring?
cards.body: Monitoring LLMs means tracking two things at once: System Health (how fast and cheap the app runs) and Semantic Health (how accurate, safe, and helpful the AI's actual words are).

cards.title: The Restaurant Critic
cards.body: Think of running a restaurant. Traditional monitoring is like checking if the kitchen is clean and the food comes out fast (latency and cost). LLM monitoring is also like hiring a food critic to taste the dishes and make sure the chef isn't accidentally serving soup that tastes like soap (hallucinations and bias).

cards.title: Monitoring in Action
cards.body: Imagine your chatbot suddenly starts giving answers in French or repeating insults. Regular servers won't flag this because the system is technically 'running fast.' Semantic monitoring catches this by reading the AI's responses and checking for weird or harmful language.

cards.title: The Green Light Illusion
cards.body: Myth: If your AI application is fast and has zero server errors, it is working perfectly. Reality: The server might be perfectly healthy, but the AI could be happily chatting nonsense to your customers!

cards.title: The Two Pillars of Tracking
cards.body: To keep your AI safe and affordable, you must look at both sides of the monitoring coin.

left.text: Tracks server speed (latency), server errors, and API costs.

right.text: Tracks truthfulness, safety, tone, and signs of bias.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following is a semantic health metric for an LLM?

options.text: How much money the API calls cost.
How fast the model generates responses.
Whether the AI is generating biased or toxic text.
Whether the server is online or offline.

exercises.explain: Speed (latency) is a system metric, whereas bias, safety, and hallucination levels are semantic metrics because they deal with the meaning of the text.

exercises.prompt: True or False: LLM monitoring requires checking both the server speed and the actual quality/truthfulness of the AI's words.
exercises.explain: Traditional software monitoring only tracks server health (like speed). LLM monitoring must also track the meaning and safety of the generated language.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the description of AI health tracking.
exercises.sentence: Checking if an AI is hallucinating or showing bias is part of tracking its ______ health.
exercises.explain: Semantic health checks focus on the quality and safety of the words the AI generates, rather than just how fast the server runs.

exercises.prompt: Identify which system response shows a clear semantic failure (hallucination):

options.text: System A: 'Abraham Lincoln was the first President of the United States and loved using smartphones.'
System B: 'George Washington was the first President of the United States, serving from 1789 to 1797.'
System C: 'I do not have enough information to confidently answer that question.'

exercises.explain: The AI in System A is hallucinating (making up fake historical details), which is a major semantic failure. System B is giving accurate historical information.

exercises.prompt: You are launching an AI customer support bot for a major retail store. Which metrics must you track to ensure the bot is working well and safe for customers?

options.text: Only system speed (latency) because AI outputs never change over time.
Only accuracy, since LLMs run on unlimited free cloud hardware.
Both operational health (cost, speed) and semantic health (hallucinations, bias).
Neither; LLMs are self-monitoring and fix their own errors automatically.

exercises.explain: Monitoring LLMs is a double-duty job: we must track hardware/operational metrics (cost, speed) alongside semantic safety metrics (hallucination, toxic language).
